title: crude style pack (OverSimplified-inspired)
short_description: Ship the crude pack complete — palette, typography, at least 12 characters with 3+ poses each, at least 20 props, blob-nation shapes, comedic label-pop animation set, all golden-tested and license-vetted — and pass the founder style checkpoint on the re-rendered Phase 1 fixture (logged).
long_description_md:
# `crude` style pack (OverSimplified-inspired)

## Why this task exists

`crude` is the first of the two launch style packs (product-spec capability 5) and the one the wedge genre — history/geopolitics explainers — is actually built around. It is the pack the founder named explicitly in the founding conversation as the thing he needs to watch once and say "yes, that's the style." This task is that checkpoint for Phase 3: MS-Paint-ish characters, blob nations, comedic label pops, in a palette and typography that reads unmistakably OverSimplified-adjacent, not generic-explainer.

This task completes **Phase 3: Paint rendering + `crude` style pack** — it is the last item in that phase's done-when list, and the phase itself is not finished until this pack ships at content bar and passes founder review.

## Roadmap fit and dependencies

- `style-pack-system` supplies the typed, versioned `StylePack` schema (palette roles, font roles, `stroke_profile`, `textures`, `transition_defaults`, asset manifest, `constraint_profile`) and the exact-version pinning/resolution machinery, plus `anima assets --json` and the `STYLE.PALETTE_VIOLATION` rule. This task populates that schema with real `crude` content and data — it does not redesign the schema.
- `paint-primitives` supplies the wobbly-stroke, texture-fill, paint-reveal, and glyph-outline-tracing primitives this pack's assets are rendered through. This task parameterizes those primitives (stroke profile values, texture choices) for the crude look — it does not implement new rasterization primitives.
- `golden-image-harness` supplies the addressable `GoldenBaseline` registry, vendored AA-aware comparator, and pinned-container generate/compare/update workflow every asset in this pack must register against.

Downstream, `flat-pack` (already spec'd) proves pack pluggability by shipping a second pack against this same schema in Phase 5; this task must not couple any renderer or verification code to the literal string `crude`.

## Scope and required content bar

Ship a production `crude` pack at an exact initial content version using the shared `StylePack` schema:

- **Palette**: a named, role-based palette (not raw hexes in scene code) capturing the OverSimplified-adjacent look — bold flat colors, limited high-contrast palette typical of the genre. Roles must cover the semantic categories scene authors need (backgrounds, primary/secondary fill roles, text-on-light/text-on-dark, accent/highlight, at minimum).
- **Typography**: bundled, license-vetted fonts appropriate to the crude/comedic register (hand-drawn or bold marker-style faces), resolved only through `font-bundling`'s package-resident manifest — no system-font lookup.
- **Stroke profile and textures**: `stroke_profile` and `textures` values tuned for the crude look (looser wobble amplitude/jitter than `flat` will use, paper/marker-ish texture choices) expressed purely as data consumed by `paint-primitives`, never as renderer forks.
- **Characters**: at least 12 distinct character entries, each with at least 3 distinct poses, all resolving to real package-local resources with complete tags, license metadata, and attribution records. MS-Paint-ish crude figure style per the product spec.
- **Props**: at least 20 distinct props (objects, UI-style overlays, comedic set-dressing typical of the genre), same resource/license/attribution completeness bar as characters.
- **Blob-nation shapes**: a set of stylized, deliberately imprecise "blob" territory/nation shapes usable as stand-ins or overlays distinct from the real geodata the Phase 4 map engine will later render — this pack ships the shape vocabulary and asset entries, not map-engine territory binding (that integration point is Phase 4's job).
- **Comedic label-pop animation set**: `transition_defaults`/animation parameterizations for the label-pop style label entrances the product spec calls out (bouncy, punchy timing), expressed through the existing stable transition/easing registry — no new animation-system backend code.
- **Golden coverage**: every character pose, every prop, blob-nation shapes, palette/typography fixtures, and the label-pop animation set must have committed baselines in the golden-image-harness registry, generated only inside the pinned container.
- **License vetting**: every bundled font and every original/procedural or licensed-for-redistribution asset must carry complete, shipped license and attribution metadata. No copied proprietary OverSimplified artwork, characters, or trademarks — the pack is genre-inspired, not a copy.

## Founder style checkpoint

Re-render the Phase 1 scripted 60-90s multi-scene fixture project (the one Phase 1's done-when criteria used to prove first-pixels rendering) under the `crude` pack with no scene-code changes beyond the pack pin, per the pluggability contract `style-pack-system` establishes. The founder reviews the resulting contact sheet and preview render and records a pass/fail judgment plus concrete observations in `docs/cofounder/LEARNINGS.md`, per the Phase 3 roadmap item: "the Phase 1 fixture project re-rendered in `crude` looks unmistakably OverSimplified-adjacent — founder style checkpoint passed (logged)." This is a genuine visual-taste judgment call, not a mechanical check, and it is done when the founder actually says so — a failed judgment becomes a tracked asset/parameter follow-up before re-review, not a rubber stamp.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Any change to the `StylePack` schema, exact-version pinning/resolution, `anima assets` command, or `STYLE.PALETTE_VIOLATION` rule — those are `style-pack-system`'s contract; this task only populates it.
- Any new rasterization primitive (wobble, texture, paint-reveal, glyph stroking) — those are `paint-primitives`'s contract; this task only parameterizes them.
- The `flat` pack and its content, Phase 5 taste/continuity rules (`PACING.*`, `CONTINUITY.*`), and per-project severity overrides — those ship in `flat-pack` against this same schema.
- Real geodata territory binding, the map engine's `BaseMap`/`Territory`/`Border`/`InvasionArrow` node types, and blob-shape-to-real-territory mapping logic — Phase 4 scope. Blob-nation shapes here are pack asset entries only.
- Golden-image harness infrastructure (container, comparator, registry/CLI commands) — consumed here, not built here.
- The final v1 15-20 minute flagship episode and the Phase 7 fresh-agent onboarding bar — this task's checkpoint is a Phase 3 style proof on a short fixture, not the launch flagship.

deliverables.description: Ship the exact-versioned `crude` StylePack: role-based palette, bundled crude typography, tuned stroke_profile/textures data, at least 12 characters with at least 3 poses each, and at least 20 props — all resolving to real package resources with complete tags, license metadata, and attribution records; no copied proprietary artwork; no system-font lookup.

Ship blob-nation shape assets and the comedic label-pop animation set (transition_defaults parameterization via the existing stable transition/easing registry, bouncy punchy timing) as real, validated pack content distinct from map-engine territory binding.

Register golden coverage for every crude-specific rendered element: palette/typography fixtures, every character pose, every prop, blob-nation shapes, and the label-pop animation set. Baselines must be addressable through the shared golden registry, generated only in the pinned container, and compared through the existing anti-aliasing-aware harness.

Pass and log the founder style checkpoint: re-render the Phase 1 scripted 60-90s fixture project under the crude pack with only the exact pack pin changed, generate contact sheet and preview, founder reviews for unmistakably OverSimplified-adjacent look, records pass/fail plus concrete observations and artifact metadata in docs/cofounder/LEARNINGS.md.

verification.steps: Run `anima verify --project tests/fixtures/phase1_multiscene/project.py --json` with the project's style-pack pin set to the exact crude pack version and confirm it passes. Generate an annotated contact sheet and low-resolution preview with `anima preview --project tests/fixtures/phase1_multiscene/project.py`. The founder reviews those artifacts against the Phase 3 roadmap bar (unmistakably OverSimplified-adjacent), records pass/fail plus concrete observations and artifact/fixture metadata in docs/cofounder/LEARNINGS.md, and any failed judgment becomes a tracked asset or parameter follow-up before re-review.
